posts.name: Aja Frost
posts.role: Sr. Director, Global Growth & Paid, HubSpot
posts.summary: HubSpot uses branded search spikes to measure channel impact when entering new markets, analyzes pages getting direct AI traffic, and runs incrementality tests by going dark in specific markets to measure true influence beyond last-click attribution.
posts.a: The branded search spike angle is so underrated. Customers who found you through Reddit or AI just vanish into direct. Incrementality tests with dark periods are where real attribution lives 🙌
posts.b: The 'go dark in specific markets' test surfaces insights that platform data never could. If the signal isn't there before checkout, you're already behind. Real attribution lives outside the dashboard 🤝

posts.name: Sam N.
posts.summary: Sam reintroduces himself to his 17,002 followers as the co-founder of goodwipes, a DTC wellness CPG brand he's been building for 12.75 years. His motto: have fun and get sh*t done. He's all about holistic wellness of the consumer.
posts.a: 12.75 years is a number I feel. Most people don't know what the 0.75 costs 🥹 Congrats on 17k, Sam.
posts.b: The 'holistic wellness of the consumer' framing in CPG is real and mostly underserved. 12.75 years building something this joyful is the whole move 🙌

posts.name: Neil Tewari
posts.summary: Conversion Agents launched last week to 1.5M+ views and more demo requests than they can run. Neil is now hiring across GTM, engineering, growth, and ops, all in-person in San Francisco.
posts.a: The 'more demo requests than we can run' problem is such an underrated signal for real PMF. Congrats to the whole team 🙌
posts.b: 1.5M views in a week and now every hire you can think of. The demand is clearly real. Rooting hard for the Conversion team 🤝

posts.name: Joanna Hyc
posts.summary: Joanna shares highlights from Gainsight Pulse 2026 Day 1, where Gainsight announced AI agents for CS teams including a Risk Analyst Agent (3 months earlier churn detection) and an Expansion Analyst Agent. The MCP integration especially excites her because it lets CSMs build custom workflows on Gainsight's data in natural language without engineering.
posts.a: The MCP piece is the one I keep coming back to. 'In natural language, without needing engineering' changes what CS can actually build 🙌
posts.b: Three months earlier on churn risk is huge. The real question is what the follow-up motion looks like when that signal fires at scale. Excited for Day 2 🤝

posts.name: Cole Lamphere
posts.summary: Cole shares a 4-stage SaaS onboarding playbook built from a near-churn experience with a 4,200 unit multifamily customer. Stage 1 is listening only. Stage 2 configures one community first, specifically picking the most skeptical region. His core lesson: onboarding is when you earn the right to ship the next feature.
posts.a: The 'configure for one community first, pick the skeptic' stage is so right. Every time someone skips to portfolio-wide rollout it ends badly. Win the skeptic first 🤝
posts.b: Honestly the Stage 4 exit where you name three metrics before handing off is everything. Renewals are won before day 90 🙌

connections.note: Hey Aja, CSM at KnoCommerce here. Your dark traffic post was exactly what I needed to read. We help DTC brands use post-purchase surveys for exactly this kind of attribution gap. Would love to connect, no agenda.
